name: Communityamerica
description_short: CommunityAmerica is a not-for-profit credit union offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and investment services with annual profit payouts to members.
description_long:
CommunityAmerica Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution that has operated as a not-for-profit credit union focused on serving its community. As of November 1, 2025, CommunityAmerica merged with UNIFY Financial Credit Union to expand its reach and services. The organization positions itself as a "truly local credit union" committed to financial well-being and community support.

The credit union offers comprehensive banking services including checking and savings accounts, share certificates (CDs) earning up to 3.60% APY, money market accounts, and IRAs. On the lending side, they provide auto loans (rates as low as 5.24% APR), mortgages, home equity lines of credit, personal loans, student loan refinancing, boat and RV loans, and business financing including SBA loans. They also issue Visa Signature credit cards and offer investment advisory services, financial planning, retirement planning, and insurance products through their agency.

CommunityAmerica distinguishes itself through its profit-sharing model: they have distributed $110 million to members over 14 years through annual profit payouts. The credit union emphasizes local decision-making, competitive rates, low fees, and community investment in nonprofits. Their mobile app has received near-perfect ratings on both the App Store and Google Play. They also offer free financial coaching and multiple calculators for loan and investment planning.

As a credit union, membership may have eligibility requirements, and the merger integration is recent (as of November 2025). While they offer competitive rates and member benefits, rates shown are promotional (3.60% APY on certificates, 5.00% APY on high-yield savings, 5.24% APR on auto loans) and actual rates may vary based on creditworthiness and account type. The organization operates primarily as a full-service financial institution rather than a specialized lender.
services: Share certificates (CDs) with rates up to 3.60% APY
Mortgages and home equity lines of credit
Personal loans and credit cards (Visa Signature with $150 bonus)
Business checking and commercial online banking
SBA loans and business lines of credit
Investment advisory and financial planning (retirement, college, estate planning)
Insurance products (auto, homeowners, life, gap coverage)
Digital payments, Zelle, and mobile banking app
pros: Profit payout program: $110 million distributed to members over 14 years through annual profit payouts
High-yield savings earning up to 5.00% APY and certificates earning 3.60% APY on 3-month or 7-month terms
Auto loans with rates as low as 5.24% APR and hybrid home equity lines with 5.24% APR first year
Mobile app with near-perfect ratings on App Store and Google Play with full branch functionality
Comprehensive loan portfolio including auto, mortgage, personal, business, student refinancing, boat/RV, and SBA loans
Not-for-profit credit union structure with emphasis on community investment and local partnerships
Free financial coaching and extensive financial planning services (retirement, college, estate, special needs planning)
cons: Recent merger (November 1, 2025 with UNIFY Financial) may create service integration issues and member confusion
Share certificates require minimum $500 deposit ($50 for Saver Certificate), which may limit accessibility for some
Membership eligibility requirements may apply, limiting access compared to traditional banks open to all customers
Promoted rates appear to be time-limited offers; actual rates vary based on creditworthiness and account type
Limited information on business account fees, monthly minimums, or overdraft policies on the website
best_for: Credit union members seeking profit-sharing benefits and competitive rates on savings and CDs
Borrowers looking for auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans from a local institution with community focus
Small business owners needing business checking, lines of credit, SBA loans, and commercial real estate financing
Individuals seeking comprehensive financial planning including retirement, college, and investment advisory services
diagnosis: CommunityAmerica is best for credit union members seeking competitive rates, profit-sharing benefits, and full-service banking with community focus. The main caveat is the recent merger integration (November 2025) and membership eligibility requirements that may apply, along with rates that vary based on creditworthiness and may not match promotional rates advertised.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
